title: Photometric Order Topology Features
summary: Encode each object's ugriz band ordering, color-sign pattern, and discrete SED-shape topology as compact categorical and numeric features.
feature_strategy: Keep raw alpha, delta, ugriz magnitudes, redshift, spectral_type, galaxy_population, and basic adjacent colors, then add target-free ordinal shape features over the five SDSS bands. Treat lower magnitude as brighter and compute per-band brightness ranks, the full ugriz ordering token, adjacent color sign bitmask, all pairwise brighter-than comparison bits, inversion counts relative to monotone blue-to-red and red-to-blue orderings, number and location of local extrema in the magnitude sequence, longest monotone run length, second-difference sign pattern, convex/concave segment counts, and coarse topology tokens combining brightest band, faintest band, adjacent sign bitmask, and redshift regime. One-hot or count/frequency encode only these unsupervised tokens from combined train/test covariates; do not use target encodings, auxiliary labels, supervised intermediate models, or row identity joins.
baseline_model_panel: Use a reusable simple panel: a class-balanced regularized multinomial linear model on standardized numeric plus one-hot tokens, a class-balanced random-forest or extra-trees model for discrete interaction sensitivity, and a class-balanced gradient-boosted tree such as LightGBM/CatBoost with the competition-recommended multiclass balancing.
model_panel_rationale: The linear model checks whether ordinal topology adds separable low-order signal, while tree models can exploit sparse order tokens and comparison interactions without turning the hypothesis into algorithm tuning.
validation_strategy: Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold on train.csv, report balanced accuracy overall and by class; fit all unsupervised token vocabularies/frequencies using fold-train plus test covariates only, then transform fold-validation rows without labels.
materialization_hint: Implement a small feature builder that takes the ordered band list [u,g,r,i,z], produces rank arrays and string/int topology tokens, applies one-hot encoding for low-cardinality tokens and frequency encoding for higher-cardinality tokens, then appends these columns to the standard raw/categorical preprocessing before running the baseline panel.
expected_signal: If useful, tree models should gain balanced accuracy especially for QSO/STAR boundary cases where absolute colors overlap but the relative band-order shape differs; diagnostics should show nontrivial importance for order_token, adjacent_sign_bitmask, extrema counts, and second-difference sign features.
risk: This overlaps partially with prior color, SED-slope, and min/max-band features because it is derived from the same ugriz sequence; the novelty is the discrete ordinal/topological representation, which may be too lossy if continuous color magnitudes already capture all signal.

autogluon_feature_transfer.source: legacy feature engineering only; model, fold, calibration, and blending logic intentionally omitted
